tried_strategies.goal: 3 hook additions: synonym map + temporal timeline + G2a importers
G1 과적합 검증 + 일반화 수정 (word-boundary verbs + deep grep _is_decision() 제거)
G1 일반화 수치 완성 — CTX 59-query + Flask + Requests 교차 검증 완전 비교표
G1 Recall@7 상한선 분석 + 0-7d 개선 — miss pattern 분류, multi-keyword 도입
stemmed BM25 + recency — fixes MAB tokenization gap
MAB ctx_v2 verdict: 2x lift + semantic gap characterized

known_facts: 2026-04-17 iter 1: vec-daemon died 2026-04-11, SessionStart `test -S $sock` didn't detect stale socket, CM+G1 ran in BM25-only silent fallback for 6 days. Fixed via python3 socket.connect() liveness probe in settings.json.
2026-04-17 iter 2: added visible `⚠ vec-daemon down` warnings to CM header + G1 decision header so future silent failures surface in every prompt injection. Validated A/B both paths.
2026-04-17 iter 3: built benchmarks/eval/g2_goldset.json (8 queries, gold top-5 labels) + g2_goldset_scorer.py with Hit@5/MRR/RankedHit@5. First rigorous G2 bench for the hook path.
2026-04-17 iter 4: shipped T1-A (BM25 + v3 tokenizer) to ~/.claude/hooks/git-memory.py search_graph_for_prompt. Goldset: Hit@5=0.62, MRR=0.52 (was 0.36), RH@5=0.25 (was 0.18).
2026-04-17 iter 5: goldset q5 keywords must match post-extract_keywords form (Korean ko_en expansion). Exposed trade-off: broad-expansion keywords favor length-ASC, focused tokens favor BM25.
2026-04-17 iter 6: shipped T1-C query-type router (>=5 keywords → length-ASC, else BM25). Beats both pure variants: Hit@5=0.75, MRR=0.53, RH@5=0.28.

2026-04-17 iter 7: latency characterized — OLD=1.41ms, BM25=3.16ms (cold max 57ms from rank_bm25 import), ROUTER=1.50ms. The original '10× latency' claim was measurement noise from single cold-start run.

2026-04-17 iter 8: codebase-memory-mcp DB was 254h (10.6 days) stale. auto-index.py outputs a HINT ('consider running mcp__codebase-memory-mcp__index_repository') but doesn't execute — Claude often ignores the hint. Fresh index via manual MCP call lifted G2 MRR +15% (0.53→0.61) with no ranking change. STRUCTURAL bug: index staleness may outweigh ranking improvements.
2026-04-17 iter 10: tiered staleness warning in auto-index.py (silent<24h / ⚠ 3-7d / ⚠⚠ 7d+). Validated via mtime backdate. Closes observability gap.

2026-04-17 iter 11: T1-B (co-occurrence synonym mining from git log) — NEGATIVE result. Top pairs on CTX's 226-commit corpus are dominated by live-inf template boilerplate (iter↔live, goal↔success, infinite↔iter). No genuine semantic synonyms surface without heavier filtering (topic stratification, MI scoring). SOTA doc's T1-B not useful on this corpus.

2026-04-17 iter 13: auto-index.py STRUCTURAL FIX — default mode now actually executes `codebase-memory-mcp cli index_repository` via subprocess (30ms total incl CLI), no longer a hint-only hook. Three modes: default=execute, --hint-only=legacy, --force=skip staleness check. Closes the iter-8 bug where Claude ignored the hint and DB went 10+ days stale silently. Output: `[AUTO-INDEX] stale (80h old) → executed: indexed (was 3.3d stale)`.
CM had hybrid BM25+vector (0.5*cos + 0.5*bm25_norm) since 2026-04-10 via vault.db + vec0 + multilingual-e5-small. Not a new feature — was pre-existing but broken.
_SYNONYM_MAP expansion: COIR/BM25/SOTA/G1/G2/eval → commit-searchable synonyms; +0.085 Recall@7 (0.661→0.746)
Temporal timeline mode: _TEMPORAL_QUERY_RE detects history/progression/timeline queries → [DECISION TIMELINE] header + date-prefixed oldest-first output
G2a 1-hop import discovery: git grep reverse importers on G2-found Python files; cost <2s per file
Remaining 7/15 misses = live-inf iter commits filtered by _OMC_ITER_RE (structural noise, intended behavior)
New practical ceiling: ~0.75-0.80 (if live-inf iter filtering relaxed: ~0.88)
G1 overfitting root cause: _is_decision() biased to CTX-domain keywords (benchmark/eval/iter/CONVERGED)
Flask commits fail _is_decision() because they use descriptive language — fixed by Pass0 gate removal
_DECISION_VERBS_RE with word-boundary regex: \badd\b does NOT match 'address' — safe to use
Pass 0 deep grep: removing _is_decision() gate allows surfacing general-project commits via keyword grep
Flask Recall@7: 0.000 → 0.667 (+0.667) after generalization fix
CTX total Recall@7: 0.169 → 0.525 (iter2) → 0.661 (iter3)
CTX 0-7d: 0.422 → 0.622 (+0.200) — compound/numeric extraction key fix
Pass0 miss root cause: long_kws ≥4-char filter missed 'git-memory' (hyphen-split), '2040' (numeric), 'CTX' (3-char)
Fix: extract_keywords() now emits compound terms + numeric tokens; Pass0 uses up to 5 grep_kws, max-count=10
Remaining 17/45 0-7d misses: ~8 live-inf iter N/∞ (topic-dedup collapse), ~5 near-duplicate commits (same topic cluster), ~4 keyword-poor
Practical ceiling for keyword-based retrieval: ~0.65-0.70 on this benchmark (structural dedup limit)
BM25 cross-domain: zero-score → stable sort → no harmful reordering for unrelated queries

homograph audit: 0.858 surface_match_only_rate on 106 labels (85.8% >> 30% threshold) — MMR dedup + cluster sig was correct priority, but surface noise dominates BM25 output → semantic rerank (bge-daemon) is architecturally necessary
MAB Competency-4 N=5: none=0.0 vs ctx=0.2 — small lift

MAB failure modes exposed: (1) BM25 misses reversal sessions when vocab shifts, (2) even WITH retrieval, LLM can default to first-fact ('log sink' case: ctx returned initial-fact, LLM answered initial state, not reversal) — this is the architectural weakness the benchmark exists to test
Implication: recency-aware ranking needed (date-boost BM25 or explicit 'most recent wins' system prompt) to pass conflict-resolution robustly
MAB failure root cause diagnosed: 'logs' vs 'logging' tokenization gap — BM25 can't match stems
retrieve_ctx_v2 = Porter stemmer + beta=0.5 recency boost → reversal session ranks ABOVE initial on failing case
Production migration decision deferred: stemming may hurt exact-match G1 benchmark (git commit subjects); needs measurement before rollout
MAB N=5: none=0.0 / ctx=0.2 / ctx_v2=0.4 — ctx_v2 stemming+recency DOUBLES accuracy

3 remaining ctx_v2 misses all share a pattern: query vocabulary ('reranker', 'embedding model', 'package manager') has ZERO token overlap with reversal content (which uses 'BGE cross-encoder', 'multilingual-e5-small', 'uv'). Pure lexical retrieval (BM25 + stems) fundamentally cannot bridge these. Only dense candidate generation (vec-daemon or bge-daemon for reranking a DENSE top-k) could rescue these.

uncertain.question: Can semantic reranking push 0-7d recall above 0.70 for structural miss cases?
uncertain.status: unmeasured — would require vec-daemon to be available + live testing

uncertain.question: Is the live-inf iter topic-dedup collapse avoidable with a different dedup strategy?
uncertain.status: structural limit — all iter commits touch .omc/ files, same topic key

wave1_plan:
APPROACH: benchmarks/eval/puac_eval.py (Tier 2 core, reusing downstream_llm_eval.py client+runner) + /api/puac-compare endpoint + homograph_audit.py + Tier 1 harnesses.
METHOD: iter1=puac_eval scaffold w/ 4-condition runner + CL/AR/PRR + N=10 smoke; iter2=N=50 real pilot; iter3=dashboard compare pane; iter4=homograph audit; iter5=Tier 1 LongMemEval+MemoryAgentBench; iter6=Tier 3 annotation.
KEY_DECISIONS: (1) fresh counterfactual API calls not stored baseline; (2) PRR via CL-AR dissociation test (AR>0.5 AND CL<0.05); (3) LLM-judge pairwise temp=0 tracks human r=0.85+.
REUSE: utility-rate.py substring AR, _ctx_telemetry.py ab_disabled, downstream_llm_eval.py get_llm_client, hf_space_dashboard FastAPI.
BUILD NEW: puac_eval.py, homograph_audit.py, tier1_longmemeval.py, tier1_memoryagentbench.py, /api/puac-compare.
ANTI: single-number PUAC (must decompose), running Tier 1 on CTX's own corpus, skipping homograph audit.

uncertain_followups.goal: T1-A: G2 search_graph_for_prompt SQL LIKE → BM25 over (name+file_path)
uncertain_followups.status: NOT a dead-end. Implementation was reverted due to three consecutive tokenizer bugs in the A/B harness — not a theoretical failure. T1-A with v3 tokenizer (snake_case + CamelCase split, alphanumeric runs like 'bm25' kept intact) shows overlap 0.43 and canonical case 'BM25 retriever lookup' lifts from 0/5 to 3/5 with BM25Retriever class surfacing at rank 3 (OLD never showed it).
open_issues: No gold-truth benchmark yet → can't claim 'better' on ambiguous cases like 'fair eval' (1/5 overlap)
Latency 1.6ms → 14-16ms (10×) — still within hook budget but a real cost
No decision on whether to ship
uncertain_followups.next_step_if_revisited: Build 15-20 query gold-truth G2 bench first; only then re-apply T1-A with v3 tokenizer and decide on latency tradeoff.
